title: Divergent Paths
subtitle: Your work and the remote have diverged — resolve the conflict

briefing.narrative:
Disaster. While you changed Monday to 'Kung Fu', Brother Guybrush changed Monday to 'Calligraphy' on the remote. You both edited the SAME LINE.

The Head Monk sighs deeply. "This is the most common pain in collaborative work. You both changed the same line. When you pull, Git cannot decide whose change wins — you'll see conflict markers."

"Resolve the conflict by choosing what Monday should be — or combine both. Edit the file, remove the conflict markers, then add and commit."

The cat watches two birds fight over the same worm. It finds the parallel amusing.
briefing.concept:
When local and remote diverge on the same file, 'git pull' creates a merge conflict.

Conflict markers look like:
<<<<<<< HEAD
your changes
=======
their changes
>>>>>>> origin/main

Resolve by editing the file to combine both changes, then 'git add' and 'git commit'.
objectives: Pull from origin to see the conflict
The file must contain 'Kung Fu' (your Monday choice)
Add and commit the resolved merge

args.content:
Monastery Weekly Schedule:
Monday: Meditation
Wednesday: Tea Ceremony

Monastery Weekly Schedule:
Monday: Calligraphy
Wednesday: Tea Ceremony
args.author: Brother Guybrush

args.content:
Monastery Weekly Schedule:
Monday: Kung Fu
Wednesday: Tea Ceremony

hints.text: Start by pulling: git pull origin main
Check the conflict: cat schedule.txt — you'll see <<<<<<< markers
Edit the file to keep both Kung Fu AND Calligraphy, remove conflict markers, then: git add schedule.txt && git commit -m "Resolve merge"
